publication.changeSummary: Misconception MCQ, canonical fill-gap, and no code task for Exception flow and stack traces; every item remains blocked from formal exams pending independent human review.

items.title: Exception flow and stack traces: reject the misconception
items.prompt: Which statement is source-aligned for Java: Beginner to Intermediate and corrects this misconception? Catching Exception at every method makes a program robust because no failure can escape.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted draft item; independent human source, technical, pedagogy, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Compare each option with both the language-specific boundary case and the cited operational model.

feedback.correct: Correct. Catch an exception only at a responsible boundary, preserve its cause and context, and let unhandled failures propagate to a policy that can act.
feedback.incorrect: That choice preserves the misconception. Catch an exception only at a responsible boundary, preserve its cause and context, and let unhandled failures propagate to a policy that can act.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction and rejects the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: A human reviewer must verify source fidelity and ensure the distractor remains plausible without teaching it as fact.

options.text: Catch an exception only at a responsible boundary, preserve its cause and context, and let unhandled failures propagate to a policy that can act.
Catching Exception at every method makes a program robust because no failure can escape.

answer.explanation: Catch an exception only at a responsible boundary, preserve its cause and context, and let unhandled failures propagate to a policy that can act.
